AN EF1 TORNADO...3 MILES LONG AND 50 YARDS WIDE...BEGAN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF BREAUX BRIDGE AND TRAVELED TO THE NORTHEAST SIDE. 50 HOMES RECEIVED MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE...INCLUDING SOME HOMES WITH PORTIONS OF ROOFS PULLED OFF. DAMAGE TO SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED. TWO TRAILER HOMES WERE FLIPPED...INCLUDING ONE WITH AN OCCUPANT INSIDE. OCCUPANT WAS NOT INJURED.

ST MARTIN PARISH EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS A MOBILE HOME FLIPPED WITH OCCUPANT INSIDE IN BREAUX BRIDGE. OCCUPANT WAS NOT INJURED. ADDITIONALLY A SEAFOOD RESTAURANT HAD PART OF ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF AND SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NECO TOWN RD... HIT A MOBLIE HOME PARK... 3 MOBLIE HOMES PUSHED OFF THEIR FOUNDATION. 6 MOBLIE HOMES HAD SIDING AND ROOFS REELED OFF. TOUCHED DOWN AGAIN ON ORANGE GROVE AVE. WHERE IT REELED OFF THE ROOF OF A LARGE BARN ALSO TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF WITH OUT BUILDINGS DESTROYED. ENDED WITH MINOR PROPERTY DAMAGE ON CAJUN DRIVE.

MICROBURST WINDS BLEW 50-75 MPH ACROSS A FOUR MILE WIDE AND TWO MILE LONG AREA BETWEEN GAUTHIER RD AND TANK FARM RD EAST OF THE LAKE CHARLES AIRPORT. AT LEAST FOUR MOBILE HOMES HAD SHINGLES PULLED OFF OF ROOFS...SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE FLIPPED OVER...BETWEEN 50-100 MOBILE HOMES HAD SKIRTING DAMAGED OR BLOWN OFF. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. DAMAGE WAS BLOWN NORTHWEST TO SOUTHEAST.

STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM AROUND THE INTERSECTION OF BIG PASTURE ROAD AND HIGHWAY 384 TO DONNA ROAD IN BIG LAKE. ONE METAL ROOF WAS REMOVED AND NUMEROUS TREE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN ALONG THE PATH. POWER LINES WERE DOWNED IN THE AREA AND FENCES WERE ALSO DAMAGED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 60-70 MPH.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 384 NEAR LUCY LANE AND SNAPPED NUMEROUS BRANCHES AND DOWNED SEVERAL SMALL TREES ALONG THE PATH. MINOR DAMAGE TO THE ROOFING OF ONE HOME OCCURRED ALONG HIGHWAY 384 AND TO A FEW HOMES ALONG CLYDES AND CLYDE OLIVERS LANES. THE TORNADO ENDED NORTH OF T JOHNS ROAD JUST EAST OF TANS ROAD. STORM SURVEY FINDS MICROBURST DAMAGE WITH THE AXIS JUST NORTH OF I-210 IN THE SOUTH PORTION OF LAKE CHARLES. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED. IN ADDITION POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED ON OAKPARK BLVD NEAR MEMORIAL HOSPITAL WHERE THE HIGHEST WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED. TOP WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 90 MPH NORTH OF 210 AND SOUTH OF UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD AND EAST OF RYAN STREET TO 3RD AVE. MAJORITY OF DAMAGE WILL BE DUE TO FALLEN TREES ON HOMES AND POWERLINES.

NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS AND EF1 TORNADO SOUTH OF JASPER. THE TORNADO STARTED JUST WEST OF HWY 96 NEAR CO RD 303 AND ENDED JUST EAST OF HWY 96 NEAR CO RD 302. THE PATH WAS AROUND 1 MILE LONG. MORE INFORMATION TO FOLLOW LATER TODAY IN A PNS WHEN THE SURVEY COMPLETE.

JASPER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS MINOR WIND DAMAGE ON THE WEST SIDE OF HWY 96. A RESIDENCE LOCATED AT 444 COUNTY ROAD 302 HAS TREES BLOWN AGAINST IT, BUT NO MAJOR DAMAGE. POWERLINES REPORTED DOWN IN THE SAME AREA.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO IN BRIDGE CITY TX. THE PATH WAS 3 MILES LONG 100 YARDS WIDE AND THE ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 75 MPH. MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES WITH WIDE SPREAD TREE DAMAGE WITH BRANCHES SNAPPED AND TREES UPROOTED. THE TORNADO STARTED ON FM1442 ON THE NORTH SIDE OF BRIDGE CITY TX AND ENDED NEAR PATILLO ROAD.
PUBLIC REPORTS TREES DOWN...SIDING OFF OF HOMES AND CEILING DAMAGE ALONG S. PATILLO RD...POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO SOUTH OF DEQUINCY. THE PATH IS CURRENTLY ESTIMATED AT NEAR 6 MILES LONG STARTING JUST WEST OF WANDA ROAD AND ENDING JUST NORTH OF HOLBROOK PARK. MAX WIDTH ESTIMATED NEAR 150 YARDS. MORE INFORMATION TO FOLLOW IN A PNS WHEN THE SURVEY IS COMPLETE LATER TODAY.

NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO TRACK STARTING JUST WEST OF HWY 171 ALONG HOFFPAUIR RD AND ENDING ALONG NEWT HODGES RD JUST EAST OF HWY 171. A PNS WITH COMPLETE INFORMATION WILL BE SENT LATER TODAY WHEN THE SURVEY IS COMPLETE.

NWS SURVEY CONFIRMS A EF1 TORNADO TRACK FROM CAMP EDGEWOOD ROAD BETWEEN GILLIS AND RAGLEY TO TEXAS EASTERN ROAD ROUGHLY 6 MILES WEST SOUTHWEST OF REEVES. STORM SURVEY CONTINUES AND A PNS WITH MORE INFORMATION WILL BE SENT LATER TODAY.

A NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN OF HWY 384 NEAR OGEA RD TO THE SOUTH WEST OF LAKE CHARLES LA. MINOR DAMAGE TO ONE HOME WITH 2 SHEDS COLLAPSED WITH A TREE UPROOTED AND BRANCHES SNAPPED UP TO ONE FOOT IN DIAMETER.

STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A NEIGHBORHOOD ALONG TALL SKY DRIVE AND RUSTIC VIEW DRIVE. N TO NW WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60 TO NEAR 70 MPH DAMAGED ONE ROOF, REMOVED ONE CAR PORT, REMOVED SKIRTING AROUND MULTIPLE MOBILE HOMES, DAMAGED SIDING TO A COUPLE OF MOBILE HOMES, DOWNED A FEW SMALL TREES AND SHURBS, AND ROLLED 2 SHEDS.
